title: Never Be Impressed By a Politician's Claim to Be "100% Pro-Life"
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman argues that pro-life supermajorities in Oklahoma have actively chosen to keep abortion legal, so adding more 'pro-lifers' is useless. Voters should stop being impressed by the label and demand candidates commit to specific abolition legislation like the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.
audiences: Pastor or church leader weighing political endorsements

questions.question: If Oklahoma has pro-life supermajorities at every level of state government, why is abortion still legal there?
questions.answer: Because the people who run the state government are deliberately choosing not to abolish it, not because they lack the votes. The label 'pro-life' has not translated into action when these politicians have full power to make law.
questions.quote: We have pro-life super majorities at every level of our state government. Whatever the pro-lifers who lead our state government want to be the law on abortion, they make the law. The reason abortion is still legal in Oklahoma is because pro-life super majorities are making a deliberate choice not to abolish abortion.

questions.question: What concrete vote shows that 'pro-life' Oklahoma legislators have actively kept abortion legal?
questions.answer: On March 11, the overwhelming majority of self-identified pro-life Republicans in the Oklahoma Senate voted to kill an abolition bill, and not a single pro-life Republican in the House even introduced one. The state's top pro-life executives offered no support either.
questions.quote: 30 of 38 pro-life Republicans in the State Senate voted to keep abortion legal on March 11. Four of the remaining eight abstained. Not one the 77 pro-life Republicans in the State House even introduced a bill to abolish abortion. The abolition of abortion has gotten zero support from pro-life Governor Kevin Stitt or pro-life Lt. Governor (and Senate President) Matt Pinnell.

questions.question: Why does the author say electing more pro-life politicians is not the solution?
questions.answer: Adding more votes to a supermajority is meaningless when that supermajority refuses to act. The fix is replacing pro-lifers with abolitionists, not padding the existing roster.
questions.quote: We don't need more pro-lifers. Adding to the supermajority makes no difference if the super majority is choosing not to abolish abortion. What we need is for pro-lifers to become abolitionists or be replaced by abolitionists. [#PLEXIT]

questions.question: Why do politicians in Oklahoma adopt pro-life rhetoric during election season?
questions.answer: Because being seen as anti-abortion is a political necessity in most Oklahoma districts, which produces a lot of pandering language voters need to learn to see through.
questions.quote: We're in an election season and politicians know that to win in the vast majority of Oklahoma districts, they have to be seen as anti-abortion. That means a lot of pandering. We need to be able to see through the pandering.

questions.question: What specific question should a voter ask a candidate to cut through pro-life pandering?
questions.answer: Ask whether they will support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act by name. Free the States provides a candidate questionnaire as a guide for these conversations.
questions.quote: Ask for more specificity: Will they support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act? Feel free to use questions from our candidate questionnaire as a guide for your conversations with prospective Representatives and Senators.

questions.question: How should a voter respond when a candidate refuses to back abolition legislation or only offers pro-life platitudes?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader weighing political endorsements
questions.answer: Withhold support. The capitol already has plenty of pro-lifers who do nothing, so what's needed are abolitionists willing to actually move legislation.
questions.quote: If they say they won't support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act or respond with platitudes about being pro-life, they're not going to help abolish abortion and don't deserve support. The capitol is already filled with pro-lifers who do nothing to abolish abortion. We need some abolitionists.

questions.question: What does the author say senators who voted to kill SB13 will face?
questions.answer: They will be personally accountable to God for keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma for another year.
questions.quote: Here's what it looked like for the Oklahoma Senate to kill SB13 and keep abortion legal in their state for another year. Each one of these Senators will stand before God and give an answer for what they've done.

related.title: 5 Principles for Abolitionist Candidates in Republican Primaries
related.reason: Companion piece on how to vet and elect actual abolitionists rather than label-only pro-lifers.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant, They're Trying to Deceive You
related.reason: Directly tied to the SB13 vote referenced in this article and exposes the same pro-life establishment obstruction.

related.title: Against Pro-Life Compromise: Responding to Denny Burk, Andrew Walker, et al.
related.reason: Extends the critique of pro-life compromise that this article applies to Oklahoma legislators.

related.title: Christian, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Reinforces the PLEXIT theme by arguing Christians should reject the pro-life label entirely in favor of abolition.

related.title: C.R. Cali: How to Exhort Legislators Who Support Abolition and Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Practical follow-up on how voters and activists should engage their legislators on abolition bills.

related.title: Abby Johnson Says She Cares More About Federal Programs Than Abolishing Abortion
related.reason: Another example of a high-profile pro-life figure prioritizing other concerns over abolition, illustrating the pandering this article warns against.

related.title: Catechism for Anti-Abolitionists, Pro-Choice and Pro-Life
related.reason: Frames the broader anti-abolitionist posture shared by the pro-life politicians criticized here.
